battles.explanation: A brown bear outweighs a lion by over 100 kg and delivers devastating paw strikes
Tigers are apex predators — their speed, claws and bite force overpower a gorilla
A hippo's massive jaws (1,821 psi) and sheer bulk overwhelm even a large crocodile
Orcas actively hunt and kill great whites — they flip them to induce tonic immobility

animal1.name: Wolverine
animal1.hint: Most ferocious animal for its size

battles.explanation: Wolverines are documented to drive wolves and even bears from their kills — pure ferocity
Polar bears are heavier, have a longer reach and are exclusively carnivorous predators
Cape buffalos kill lions regularly — their horns and charge deliver devastating force

animal2.name: Jaguar
animal2.hint: Strongest bite of all big cats

battles.explanation: The saltwater croc's 3,700 psi bite and armoured hide are too much for a jaguar
Honey badgers are nearly immune to cobra venom and aggressively attack snakes head-on
African elephants weigh 3× more and use tusks and trunk as devastating weapons
The Komodo's venomous bite and serrated teeth give it the edge over a constrictor

animal1.name: Golden Eagle
animal1.hint: Hunting bird that takes foxes and deer fawns

animal2.name: Peregrine Falcon
animal2.hint: Fastest animal on Earth in a dive

battles.explanation: Golden eagles are far larger with talons capable of killing full-grown foxes
Anacondas are documented to constrict and swallow caimans whole in the Amazon
Hyenas regularly steal kills from cheetahs — their jaws crack bone and they far outmuscle cheetahs

animal1.name: Moose
animal1.hint: Largest member of the deer family

battles.explanation: In a 1v1 fight, a moose kick can shatter bone — wolves only win through pack tactics
Sperm whales actively hunt giant squid at extreme depths — proven by squid beaks in their stomachs
The cassowary's 10 cm razor-sharp claws can deliver disemboweling kicks in milliseconds
Leopards are apex predators that actively hunt primates — chimps flee from them
A silverback gorilla's extraordinary strength has been documented defeating leopards
In shallow coastal waters, the saltwater croc's armour and raw power dominate
A grizzly outweighs a tiger by up to 200 kg and can absorb blows a tiger cannot — most experts give the edge to the bear
In a 1v1, lions dominate hyenas — their size and power advantage is decisive outside a pack

animal2.name: Jaguar
animal2.hint: Strongest bite of all big cats

battles.explanation: In or near water, an anaconda ambushes and constricts a jaguar before it can react

animal1.name: African Wild Dog
animal1.hint: 80% hunt success rate — highest of any predator

battles.explanation: Cheetahs almost never fight — wild dogs are heavier, far more aggressive, and regularly steal cheetah kills

animal2.name: Black Mamba
animal2.hint: World's fastest and most feared snake

battles.explanation: Honey badgers are partially immune to black mamba venom and routinely kill and eat mambas
The mako outweighs and outsizes a bull shark, and its speed gives it a decisive first-strike advantage

animal1.name: Wolverine
animal1.hint: Most ferocious animal for its size

battles.explanation: Wolverines have been documented driving mountain lions from their kills — their ferocity and pain tolerance are unmatched
A Komodo's venomous bite causes rapid blood loss — a wounded boar rarely survives long enough to fight back
A king cobra's neurotoxic venom kills a Nile monitor in minutes — the lizard has no real defence
Out of water, a cape buffalo's horns and sheer bulk overwhelm a crocodile — crocs typically release rather than risk injury
Siberian tigers are significantly larger and heavier than lions — in historical combats, tigers frequently won

animal2.name: Moose
animal2.hint: Largest member of the deer family

battles.explanation: Grizzlies are documented moose killers — a bear's crushing bite and sheer power overwhelm even a massive bull moose

animal2.name: Walrus
animal2.hint: Tusked giant of the Arctic seas

battles.explanation: Adult walruses weigh up to 1,700 kg — more than triple a polar bear — and their tusks inflict lethal wounds
In shallow coastal water, the croc's armour and ambush bite overpower a tiger shark out of its element

animal1.name: Wolverine
animal1.hint: Most ferocious animal for its size

battles.explanation: Wolverines regularly compete with and displace lynx — their aggression and toughness make them superior fighters
A bull elephant outweighs a hippo by 4,000 kg and uses tusks and raw mass to dominate — hippos give way to elephants at waterholes
Chimpanzees are heavier, stronger and more combat-experienced — their group fighting instincts give them the decisive edge
Snow leopards are built for ambush in their mountain terrain — they overpower wolves in one-on-one encounters
A Komodo's venomous bite and armoured hide absorb a cassowary's kicks — the dragon's patience and venom win the fight
Nile crocs share rivers with bull sharks and dominate in shallow, murky water — their bite force and armour are unmatched
A gorilla's crushing grip delivers 800 kg of force — a honey badger's ferocity can't compensate for the massive size difference
In deep open water, the great white's speed, size and bite force overwhelm a crocodile away from its coastal territory
On land, a lion's speed and coordinated attack overcome a crocodile — recorded instances show lions killing crocs at waterholes

animal1.name: Bison
animal1.hint: North America's largest land animal — up to 900 kg

battles.explanation: A bison outweighs a grizzly 3-to-1 — its charge and horns deliver devastating force that even a bear cannot absorb
A mountain lion ambushes from above and delivers a killing bite to the skull — in documented 1v1 encounters, the cougar dominates
Lions weigh twice as much as leopards — leopards flee from lions and lose every direct confrontation
A hippo weighs 3,000 kg versus 800 kg for a buffalo — its bone-crushing jaws and sheer mass settle this quickly

animal1.name: Jaguar
animal1.hint: Strongest bite of all big cats

battles.explanation: Jaguars have evolved a skull and bite force specifically adapted to pierce caiman skulls — they are documented caiman killers
Grizzlies outweigh black bears by 200 kg and are far more aggressive — black bears almost always retreat from grizzlies
A lone adult sperm whale outweighs an orca 10-to-1 — its massive tail fluke and crushing jaw make it more than a match in a 1v1

animal2.name: Gaboon Viper
animal2.hint: Longest fangs of any snake — up to 5 cm

battles.explanation: King cobras actively hunt and eat other snakes — they are faster, longer and their venom is specifically lethal to reptiles

animal2.name: Bobcat
animal2.hint: North America's most widespread wild cat

battles.explanation: Mountain lions are three times heavier than bobcats — in any direct encounter, the outcome is not in doubt

animal2.name: Wolverine
animal2.hint: Most ferocious animal for its size

battles.explanation: Snow leopards weigh three times as much as wolverines — their speed and killing bite far outweigh the wolverine's ferocity

animal2.name: Baboon
animal2.hint: Highly aggressive primate with long canines

battles.explanation: Chimpanzees are stronger, heavier and far more combat-capable — their group fighting instinct translates to brutal 1v1 efficiency
In water, a Nile croc's 2,000 psi bite and death roll tear through anaconda muscle before constriction can take effect

animal1.name: Harpy Eagle
animal1.hint: World's most powerful eagle — talons like a grizzly's claws

animal2.name: Golden Eagle
animal2.hint: Hunting bird that takes foxes and deer fawns

battles.explanation: Harpy eagles are twice the weight of golden eagles with talons 10 cm long — they are the undisputed apex raptor
Hippos are 700 kg heavier and their bite force exceeds 1,800 psi — rhinos typically yield at shared waterholes

animal1.name: American Alligator
animal1.hint: Ancient apex predator of the American south

battles.explanation: Nile crocodiles grow significantly larger and have a stronger bite — in documented comparisons, they dominate American alligators

animal1.name: African Wild Dog
animal1.hint: 80% hunt success rate — highest of any predator

animal2.name: Baboon
animal2.hint: Highly aggressive primate with long canines

battles.explanation: Wild dogs are relentless and coordinated — a lone baboon's canines can injure but not stop a determined wild dog
King cobras eat pythons — their neurotoxic venom immobilises the python's muscles before it can complete a constriction

animal1.name: Wolverine
animal1.hint: Most ferocious animal for its size

battles.explanation: Wolverines have extra-tough skin and attack from the face where quills are sparse — they are one of the few predators that successfully hunt porcupines
Near water, an anaconda ambushes and begins constriction before a puma can react — documented kills of large cats have been recorded
Komodo dragons reach 3 m and 90 kg versus 1.5 m for most monitors — their venom and sheer bulk decide the outcome immediately

comparisons.explanation: A cheetah hits 120 km/h — 50% faster than a sprinting lion
The pronghorn is North America's fastest land animal at 89 km/h — evolved to outrun now-extinct predators
The peregrine falcon dives at 389 km/h — the fastest recorded movement in the entire animal kingdom
The sailfish reaches 110 km/h — it's the fastest fish in the ocean
An ostrich can sustain 60 km/h for long distances — faster than most horses in a sprint
Red kangaroos can hit 71 km/h in short bursts — nearly as fast as a cheetah's cruising speed
Adult hippos reach 3,000 kg — making them the world's third-heaviest land animal after elephants and rhinos
Polar bears average 500 kg — nearly double a typical grizzly
Saltwater crocodiles reach 700 kg — nearly 9 times heavier than the world's largest lizard
A bull moose reaches 600 kg — 6 times heavier than the largest jaguar
The saltwater croc bites at 3,700 psi — nearly 6× the bite force of a great white shark
A hippo's bite at 1,821 psi can cut a crocodile in half — nearly 3× stronger than a lion
A jaguar bites at 1,500 psi — the strongest bite relative to body size of all big cats
Greenland sharks live 400+ years — some alive today were born before Mozart
Giant tortoises regularly reach 150+ years — the verified record is 187 years
The oldest verified koi — Hanako — lived to 226 years old in Japan

comparisons.questionText: Who's stronger relative to body size?
comparisons.explanation: The dung beetle lifts 1,141× its body weight — the strongest animal on Earth relative to size
A silverback gorilla can lift up to 800 kg — 4× more than the strongest chimpanzee
The mantis shrimp's punch accelerates like a bullet — it can break aquarium glass
Sperm whales reach 2,000 m and hold their breath for up to 90 minutes

sliders.question: How fast (km/h) can a peregrine falcon dive?
sliders.explanation: The peregrine falcon dives at 389 km/h — the fastest movement ever recorded in the animal kingdom

sliders.question: How many times its own body weight can a dung beetle lift?
sliders.explanation: The dung beetle lifts 1,141× its body weight — the strongest animal on Earth relative to size

sliders.question: How deep (in metres) can a sperm whale dive?
sliders.explanation: Sperm whales reach 2,000+ metres, holding their breath for up to 90 minutes

sliders.question: At what age does a Greenland shark reach sexual maturity? (years)
sliders.explanation: Greenland sharks mature around 150 years old — making them the slowest-maturing vertebrate on Earth

sliders.question: How many kg can an African elephant lift with its trunk?
sliders.explanation: An elephant's trunk can lift 270 kg and contains over 40,000 muscles

sliders.question: How heavy (in kg) is an adult male polar bear?
sliders.explanation: Adult male polar bears average 500 kg — nearly double a grizzly bear, making them the world's largest land carnivore

sliders.question: What is the bite force (in psi) of a saltwater crocodile?
sliders.explanation: The saltwater croc bites at 3,700 psi — the strongest bite force ever measured, nearly 6× that of a great white shark

sliders.question: How fast (km/h) can a cheetah run at top speed?
sliders.explanation: A cheetah reaches 120 km/h in short bursts — it accelerates from 0 to 100 km/h in just 3 seconds

sliders.question: How many volts can an electric eel discharge?
sliders.explanation: Electric eels can discharge up to 860 volts — enough to stun a horse or kill smaller animals outright

sliders.question: How many times per second does a hummingbird flap its wings?
sliders.explanation: A ruby-throated hummingbird beats its wings ~53 times per second — creating the characteristic humming sound
